2605.21467.title: DelTA: Discriminative Token Credit Assignment for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
2605.21467.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a central technique for improving the reasoning capabilities of large language models. Despite its effectiveness, how response-level rewards translate into token-level probability changes remains poorly understood. We introduce a discriminator view of RLVR updates, showing that the policy-gradient update direction implicitly acts as a linear discriminator over token-gradient vectors and thereby determines which token probabilities are increased or decreased during learning. Under standard sequence-level RLVR, this discriminator is constructed from positive- and negative-side centroids formed by advantage-weighted averaging of token-gradient vectors. However, such centroid construction can be dominated by shared high-frequency patterns, such as formatting tokens, diluting sparse yet discriminative directions that better distinguish high-reward responses from low-reward ones. To address this limitation, we propose $\textbf{DelTA}$, a discriminative token credit assignment method that estimates token coefficients to amplify side-specific token-gradient directions and downweight shared or weakly discriminative ones. These coefficients reweight a self-normalized RLVR surrogate, making the effective side-wise centroids more contrastive and thereby reshaping the RLVR update direction. On seven mathematical benchmarks, DelTA outperforms the strongest same-scale baselines by 3.26 and 2.62 average points on Qwen3-8B-Base and Qwen3-14B-Base, respectively. Additional results on code generation, a different backbone, and out-of-domain evaluations further demonstrate the generalization ability of DelTA.
2605.21467.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習從可驗證獎勵（RLVR）已成為提高大型語言模型推理能力的核心技術。儘管其有效性如何，響應級獎勵如何轉化為標記級概率變化仍然了解不足。我們引入了RLVR更新的判別器視角，顯示出政策梯度更新方向隱含地作為標記梯度向量的線性判別器，從而決定在學習過程中哪些標記概率被增加或減少。在標準序列級RLVR下，這個判別器是由通過優勢加權平均標記梯度向量形成的正側和負側質心構建的。然而，這種質心構建可能會被共享的高頻模式主導，例如格式化標記，稀疏但具區分性的方向因此被稀釋，這些方向更能區分高獎勵響應和低獎勵響應。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了$\textbf{DelTA}$，一種判別性標記信用分配方法，該方法估算標記係數以放大側特定的標記梯度方向並降低共享或弱區分性方向的權重。這些係數重新加權自正規化的RLVR替代品，使得有效的側向質心更加對比，從而重塑RLVR更新方向。在七個數學基準上，DelTA在Qwen3-8B-Base和Qwen3-14B-Base上分別比最強的同尺度基準高出3.26和2.62個平均點。關於代碼生成、不同骨幹和域外評估的額外結果進一步展示了DelTA的泛化能力。
2605.21467.author: Kaiyi Zhang et.al.
2605.21467.authors: Kaiyi Zhang, Wei Wu, Yankai Lin
